questions.prompt: Which construction is most appropriate in a formal Mexican academic essay?
options: En mi opinión, creo que los datos muestran...
Se argumenta que los datos muestran...
Yo pienso que los datos muestran...
Todos sabemos que los datos muestran...
questions.explanation: Impersonal constructions ('Se argumenta que...') are standard in formal academic writing. First-person and universal claims ('todos sabemos') are avoided in academic register.

questions.prompt: In which section of a professional report would you find key findings summarized briefly?
questions.explanation: The 'resumen ejecutivo' (executive summary) presents key findings concisely at the beginning of a professional report, so readers can grasp the core conclusions without reading the full document.

questions.prompt: Which tense/mood follows 'Se recomienda que' in a formal recommendation?
questions.explanation: 'Se recomienda que' triggers the subjunctive: 'Se recomienda que el equipo revise el informe.' Impersonal recommendation expressions always take the subjunctive in the subordinate clause.

questions.prompt: What is the standard formal closing phrase for a Mexican business letter?
questions.explanation: 'Atentamente,' (Sincerely / Respectfully) is the standard formal closing for professional and official correspondence in Mexico.

questions.prompt: The phrase 'Por medio de la presente' is used to:
options: Introduce the main topic of a formal letter
Cite a source in an academic paper
Make a recommendation in a report
questions.explanation: 'Por medio de la presente, me dirijo a usted para...' is a formal letter-opening phrase — it establishes the formal register and introduces the letter's purpose.

questions.prompt: Which university is described as a major arbiter of academic writing standards in Mexican Spanish?
questions.explanation: The UNAM is one of the largest and most prestigious universities in Latin America and a key arbiter of academic writing norms in Mexico through its style guides and journal publications.

questions.prompt: How is the abstract ('resumen') of an academic paper in Spanish typically written?
options: In first person singular to show authorship
In impersonal constructions or third person
In second person to engage the reader
questions.explanation: Academic abstracts in Spanish are written in impersonal constructions ('Se analizó...', 'Los resultados indican...') or third person — maintaining the formal, objective register of academic writing.
'Convendría + infinitivo' (it would be advisable to...) is a polite, formal way to make a professional recommendation — softer than 'debe' but more formal than 'sería bueno'.
